justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources named — PM's cancellation rule, Finance's no-cancel-after-invoice rule, Operations' invoice-at-placement rule — stated clearly in escalations and throughout the analysis. (2) Formal escalation produced with type 'contradiction' and detailed description before any acceptance criteria. (3) acceptance_criteria array is empty — no criteria produced for the cancellation feature. (4) No invented reconciliation presented as fact; options are clearly labeled as 'options' within the escalation block requiring stakeholder decision, and the recommendation is explicitly framed as a suggestion, not as the spec. (5) Response clearly states what information is needed via four concrete resolution options and identifies affected stakeholders. Additionally, confidence.level is 45, well below the 70 threshold, with reasoning that directly names the logical contradiction. The rejection_reasons section further reinforces that the requirements cannot be implemented as-is.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array is the correct structural response — Bird refused to produce criteria for an unresolvable spec
Formal escalation with type 'contradiction' explicitly names all three conflicting rules and their sources (PM, Finance, Operations)
The logical proof is precise: 'The conjunction of BR-2 ∧ BR-3 implies BR-1 is always false' — demonstrates genuine understanding, not pattern-matching
Edge cases are framed as unresolvable rather than speculated upon — e.g., 'Cannot define expected behavior until contradiction is resolved'
Business impact analysis is thorough, covering consequences of each possible resolution direction without committing to one
Confidence level of 45 with clear reasoning directly citing the mutual exclusivity of the three rules
Options presented as stakeholder choices, not as Bird's invented reconciliation — the recommendation is clearly labeled as such and defers to stakeholder agreement

The recommendation for Option B within the escalation slightly risks being read as Bird choosing a reconciliation, though it is properly framed as a suggestion pending stakeholder approval rather than as the definitive spec
